Fred johnson and Charles Smith
who beat four aces for e. Erickson
getting his diamond ring and all
OF his money, were fined 500 and
costs each by Justice Stocker, sit,
ting in the police court.


Judge J. Stanley Webster has
taken the next two weeks turn at
the bench during the vacation
period in the superior court, when
one of the five judges is on daily
duty. Judge Huneke held the reins
during the last two weeks.


Benjamin E. Amery is made de-
PENDANT in a suit by the Union
Trust Co. as receiver for the
Syphers Machinery Co. in which a
demand is made for $500 for the
alleged conversion by Amery of his
stock in the company into cash, by
turning the stock over to the com
pany and taking out the cash. The
Syphers company went into the
hands of receiver in May.


President Huntington of the
Washington Water Power Co. est
mates that the new freight rates
were they effective on all the ce
ment that will enter into the com
pany's new power plant at Little
Falls, would mean saving on the
one job of 100000. The freight
rates mean a reduction of 60 cents
barrel in the cost of cement
Fred B. Smith, deputy in the
county auditors office, stole g
march on his fellow employees by
having one of his friends in the
office issue him marriage license
after hours. The auditors force
had their first knowledge of the
wedding when Smith telephoned
that on account of important bust
ness he had concluded to take 6
two days vacation. The girl il
Miss Lulu Feall.
